response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, ensuring that each participant has the opportunity to engage in a meaningful activity. She divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To provide a structured and engaging session, Emily prepares 3 writing prompts for each group. This means she needs 3 prompts × 4 groups = 12 initial writing prompts. 

To accommodate the possibility that groups may want to continue writing after finishing the initial set, Emily also prepares an additional set of 10 extra prompts. These prompts are shared equally among all the groups, so each group can access an equal number of extra prompts if needed. 

In addition to the writing prompts, Emily also maintains a library of writing resources and encourages the residents to explore different styles and themes. She ensures that the library is well-stocked and accessible to all participants. 

Emily’s thoughtful preparation includes both the initial and extra writing prompts, as well as maintaining a library of materials to support the creative process. 

The total number of writing prompts Emily needs to prepare is 12 (initial) + 10 (extra) = 22 writing prompts. 

The END.
